essence: Installs fire‑stopping materials to seal penetrations and maintain fire‑resistant barriers within buildings.

identity.title: Fire Stopping Installer
identity.summary: Applies fire‑resistant sealants, boards, wraps, and barriers around pipes, cables, ducts, and structural openings to prevent fire and smoke spread.

salary.notes: Installers specialising in large commercial or high‑rise projects can exceed £45k-£55k+.

qualifications: Training in fire‑stopping and passive fire‑protection installation
Knowledge of fire‑resistant materials and certification requirements
Ability to read construction drawings and follow specifications
Understanding of building regulations and fire‑resistance standards

lifestyle.description: Fire stopping installers work on construction sites and refurbishments applying fire‑resistant materials to seal penetrations and maintain compartmentation. The role suits individuals who enjoy practical work, construction environments, and contributing to life‑safety compliance.

habits: Installs fire‑stopping materials accurately and neatly
Follows strict safety and compliance standards
Keeps detailed photographic and written records
Communicates clearly with site managers and inspectors
Checks materials and installation quality carefully
